title: Enterprise Architect
description_html: Your work days are brighter here.     We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.           About the Team  At Workday, we believe that passion and customer focus are key to the business of enterprise applications. We run our company with a core value of fun in mind every day, working hard while fostering an environment where people truly believe in what they are doing. A significant part of the Workday Experience involves providing people with the opportunity to grow and develop their careers, hopefully lifelong ones, at Workday. The Presales leadership team strives to create a culture of opportunity where employees can grow their careers while making amazing contributions to our company and our customers.    About the Role        Workday is seeking a highly passionate Technology Solution Consultant, aligned as an Enterprise Architect, to join the Solution Consulting (‘presales’) team. This role combines deep technical expertise with communication, innovation and creativity to help customers envision their future with Workday.     What You’ll Do      • Articulates and differentiates the Workday Core Architecture (intelligent data core) to both technical and business buyer personas, showcasing Workday's foundational technology.       • Strategically lead high-level discussions on Workday's AI, Machine Learning (ML), and Agent-based architectures, positioning our solution as the future-ready, intelligent cloud platform.      • Craft compelling presentations that showcase why Workday’s technology is fundamentally different and deliver impactful demonstrations that bridge complex technology with clear business outcomes.      • Dive deep into technical topics such as data security, reporting & analytics, integrations, and Workday Extend, illustrating how these components support intelligent automation and extensibility. Workday's platform capabilities, specifically concerning Workday Build (leveraging Workday Extend and low-code/no-code tools) and Workday Data Cloud (addressed primarily through the capabilities of Apache Iceberg and Prism Analytics), are integral to demonstrating the platform's value and differentiation.      • Collaborate with stakeholders to create technical as-is and to-be architecture designs using graphical discovery tools, specifically highlighting how the Workday platform simplifies the IT landscape and drives value.      • Conducts discoveries, deep dives, and scripted demonstrations with little support, and conducts discoveries and answers RFP questions independently.      • Attain advanced domain knowledge and industry knowledge. Differentiates Workday product features and understands the competitive landscape, articulating customer use cases and value realizations to prospects.      • Communicate complex technical concepts effectively to functional and technical audiences, while engaging confidently with C level executives.      • Proactively cultivates relationships with sales teams and the broader Presales organization.      • Demonstrates strong business operator skills (e.g., documentation, notes, knowledge sharing, salesforce hygiene, event manager updates, etc.).      About You      What We Are looking for   - Exceptional presentation and demonstration skills.     - Collaborative spirit and excellent communication skills.     - Expertise in technical areas such as cloud architecture, integrations, data security, and reporting tools.     - Experience in Gen AI tools, LLMs and Agent-building tools, with a passion for technical innovation.     - Ability to create and present technical architectures    - A customer-focused mindset with a track record of delivering tailored solutions.     - A fast learner who shows adaptability in fast moving organisations     - An innate desire to showcase the role of technology in achieving business outcomes           Basic Qualifications:      3+ years experience in enterprise software technical solution consulting (“Presales”), solution experience, implementation consulting, solution architecture, technical account management or similar relevant experience      3+ years experience delivering compelling technical presentations and demonstrations      3+ years experience leading discovery conversations and confidence in delivering impactful demonstrations to C-Suite executives, business leaders and technical evaluators.       3+ years experience working with common cloud architectures, security, reporting, and/or integration standards.      Experience or certifications in solution architecture on hyperscaler environments such as AWS or GCP, is a preferred qualification      Exposure to AI agents and experience in agent development tools such as LangGraph, Flowise, Dify, AutoGPT, Vertex AI        Possessing familiarity with low-code platforms and tools, along with hands-on experience designing and deploying applications or workflows to drive business efficiency is a preferred qualification      Understanding of IT landscapes and ability to solution evolutions from current to future state architectures       Experience developing or supporting Financial or HCM applications, customisations, reports, workflow, BI, integration, analytics or being involved in implementations, upgrades or re-implementations is an added advantage.          Other Qualifications:      Existing knowledge of Workday application and technical architecture is beneficial, but if you have experience on legacy systems or our competitor’s systems, we’ll train you on Workday      Experience with AI/ML concepts, tools, or frameworks and their application in business systems, automation, or analytics.      App or web development experience is beneficial. Working knowledge of HTML, Javascript,  CSS, C+, Python or other is a plus.      Familiarity with basic networking principles such as VPCs, firewalls, load balancers and related technologies      Working knowledge of information security frameworks, security standards and guidelines is beneficial      Familiarity with web service integration standards such as WSDL, SOAP, JSON and ability to use SFTP and UNIX through a command line interface is helpful.      Experience with the value selling paradigm is a plus          Our Approach to Flexible Work      With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply   spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field   with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.     At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email     accommodations@workday.com    .         Are you being referred to one of our roles? If so, ask your connection at Workday about our Employee Referral process!    At Workday, we value our candidates’ privacy and data security.  Workday will never ask candidates to apply to jobs through websites that are not Workday Careers.       Please be aware of sites that may ask for you to input your data in connection with a job posting that appears to be from Workday but is not.      In addition, Workday will never ask candidates to pay a recruiting fee, or pay for consulting or coaching services, in order to apply for a job at Workday.

Who We Are:                                                                                                     Hewlett Packard Enterprise is the global edge-to-cloud company advancing the way people live and work. We help companies connect, protect, analyze, and act on their data and applications wherever they live, from edge to cloud, so they can turn insights into outcomes at the speed required to thrive in today’s complex world. Our culture thrives on finding new and better ways to accelerate what’s next. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. We are redefining what’s next and combining our legacy of innovation with a bold new goal to accelerate everything we do. Our sales organization is comprised of 10,000+ professionals in , presales, service, and support functions. Together with our partners, we deliver global, commercial, public sector & small/medium business customers throughout 11 geographies. We’re transforming businesses. Join us to redefine what’s next for you. Advise Sales Engineers and partners on the recommended technical approach and solution.  Responsible for the architecture of end-to-end Networking Infrastructure solutions with deep technical expertise in all aspects of networking. Drive Network architecture and designs to ensure customers' business and technical requirements are met.  In a complex Networking Infrastructure deal, the Senior Principal Architect will be responsible for the end-to-end technical solution & customizations orchestrating other Specialists and Technical resources and including Systems Engineers as well as Product Management.  As a Senior Principal Architect, you will review and provide insights based on HW and SW technical configurations for the solution addressing the customer requirements and ensuring successful project implementation.  Develop deep partnerships with Integrators, and other key partners to design solutions to solve emerging and complex problems for customers across Industries, workloads.  Architect and design Hyperscale Network Solutions that deliver against customers' requirements and business challenges.  Excel in delivering Demos and Proof of Concept on solutions as well as clearly articulating the value proposition aligned to customer use case.  Translate customer business and functional objectives to technical solution requirements.  Shape/customize solutions across the entire company portfolio (products, services, solutions) with necessary third-party components and offered as either CAPEX or SaaS, and IaaS. Close alignment with product management.  Work with Sales to articulate technical value, present solutions, and overcome customer objections.  Build relationships with senior resources and technical decision makers with key customers  Ensure smooth knowledge transfer and hand-over to the delivery team.  Sizing, design, configuration, and pricing of technical solutions, using sizing and industry best practices.      What You Need To Bring    Advanced degree in technology or related field preferred, or equivalent technical qualifications.  12+ years of technical experience in Network Infrastructure Design with a focus on technical consulting and solutions selling.  5+ Industry standard relevant technology certifications or equivalent experience expected.  Hyperscale Network architecture frameworks and project management methodologies and certifications are desirable.  Working knowledge of hands-on with JunOS, EOS, IOS – certifications preferred.  Strong resource management skills, including how and when to effectively engage SMEs/specialists/Engineering resources.  Hands-on experience with multiple Networking products, solutions, tools, or services aligned to respective job responsibility.  Ability to design and develop a playbook for demonstrations or walkthroughs of products, solutions, tools, or services.  Ability to deliver live demonstrations or walkthroughs of products, solutions, tools, or services to customers, partners, and other stakeholders.  Unique mastery of partner offerings and how/when to leverage them for deals within an area of specialization.  Professional with strong business acumen and general management guidance abilities.  Ability to build relationships with technical decision makers and C-level executives in client organizations.  Experience preparing RFP/Tender response documents like compliance to the RFP, bill of materials, commercial/pricing, and solution documents to drive a successful response.      Additional Skills    Arabic speaking is highly desirable  Formal or informal technical sales leadership or mentoring is desired.  Certifications such as JNCIE or equivalent are desired.  Programming - exposure and familiarity with API's, Postman, JSON, Python, etc. is desired.  This position may involve up to 50% travel, including the opportunity for international travel a few times a year on short notice.  Accountability, Active Learning, Active Listening, Assertiveness, Bias, Building Rapport, Buyer Personas, Coaching, Complex Sales, Creativity, Critical Thinking, Cross-Functional Teamwork, Customer Experience Strategy, Customer Interactions, Design Thinking, Empathy, Financial Acumen, Follow-Through, Growth Mindset, Identifying Sales Opportunities, Industry Knowledge, Intellectual Curiosity, Long Term Planning, Managing Ambiguity.      The Sales Engineering Team  The Sales Engineering team enables us to sell and support sophisticated networking and security solutions. If you have over-the-top technical capability and curiosity; if you can influence both technical and business partners by translating business goals and challenges into technical solutions while differentiating us from competing solutions; if your interpersonal and high-impact presentation skills evoke passion and confidence; if you’re a born problem-solver that loves to get into the details and have a burning desire to answer the hard problems in a constantly evolving, results-oriented industry - this is the place for you!     Minimum Qualifications    Extensive experience working in a pre-sales role selling networking equipment and/or solutions.  Ability to communicate, both oral and written, on complex technical and sales issues to a variety of audiences, including executive-level technical decision-makers.  Ability to make and communicate relevant connections between proposed technical solutions and customers' business requirements.  Strong understanding and knowledge of how to build successful relationships between an equipment manufacturer and large customers or partners.  12+ years relevant industry experience in Cloud/AI Networking Infrastructure, routing and switching domains. The candidate should have experience in multiple of the following technical domains:  Expert level hands-on design and architecture leadership of GPU/AI cluster networking, including lossless Ethernet fabrics (RoCEv2, PFC, ECN, DCQCN), InfiniBand, and InfiniBand-to-Ethernet interoperability for high-performance compute environments.  Hands-on experience and architectural leadership in AI Data Center fabric design, including 400G/800G interconnects, non-blocking leaf-spine and rail-optimized topologies, and congestion control tuning for distributed AI/ML training workloads.  Hands-on expertise and architectural leadership with AIOps and AI-driven network operations platforms such as Mist AI, Marvis, and Apstra, including intent-based networking, anomaly detection, predictive insights, and closed-loop automation.  Deep hands-on design and architecture experience scaling LLM and GenAI training cluster infrastructure, including collective communication patterns (NCCL, RCCL), GPU-to-GPU east-west traffic optimization, and high-throughput storage networking for AI workloads.  Working knowledge of NVIDIA reference architectures (DGX SuperPOD, Spectrum-X), AI Data Center solutions (AI-Optimized Ethernet, QFX/PTX for AI), and competitive AI networking offerings.  Expert level and hands-on knowledge of designing large scale IP/MPLS based Access/Metro/Edge and Core networks, MPLS Traffic Engineering.  Expert level and hands-on knowledge of designing large scale Datacenters IP Fabrics, EVPN/VXLAN Fabrics, DCI solutions.  Expert level and hands-on knowledge of SDN environments including Overlay protocols such as MP-BGP, EVPN and tunneling technologies such as VXLAN, MPLS, SR/SRv6. Virtualization technologies on x86 and scaling using technologies such DPDK, SR-IOV, SmartNICs etc.  Open Networking switch hardware (white box) and related software (SONiC, Cumulus, BigSwitch etc.).  Formal or informal technical sales leadership or mentoring is desired.  Certifications such as JNCIE or equivalent are desired. Who We Are:                                                                                                     Hewlett Packard Enterprise is the global edge-to-cloud company advancing the way people live and work. We help companies connect, protect, analyze, and act on their data and applications wherever they live, from edge to cloud, so they can turn insights into outcomes at the speed required to thrive in today’s complex world. Our culture thrives on finding new and better ways to accelerate what’s next. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. description_html: The Company     PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.     We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.    We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards.  Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.     Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day.  We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities. This outstanding opportunity allows you to work with modern technology, enabling researchers, developers, and AI infrastructure providers worldwide. What you'll be doing:     Develop and expand comprehensive workflows for the ISV and NCP partner validation program, handling engagement, qualification, onboarding, test execution, and result publication across multiple partners simultaneously. description_html: Your work days are brighter here.     We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.           About the Team  As a member of our North America Presales team, you will sit at the exciting intersection of Product Management, Consulting, Marketing, and Value Management, gaining unique exposure across the entire business. In this role, you’ll do more than just present software solutions; you’ll communicate and demonstrate how Workday’s applications bring the future of work to life for our prospects and customers.  Our team is defined by a mission-driven commitment to creating a brighter workday for all, fueled by a high-performance culture where we balance accountability for results with a genuine sense of fun and mutual support. We achieve collaborative impact by working hand-in-hand with our sales counterparts to drive growth through integrity and innovation, consistently showcasing the transformative power of the Workday platform. Ultimately, we foster an environment of continuous growth, empowering you to bring your best self to work and sharpen your skills alongside a diverse and highly skilled group of peers.  By joining this team, you become a vital storyteller and strategist, helping us maintain our momentum by illustrating the tangible, transformative value of our solutions.    About the Role      Enterprise Architects are our top customer-facing technical experts. In this role, you will serve as a trusted advisor to Workday customers and prospects, serving as a key customer-facing expert on the under-the-hood technology, architecture, and security for some of our most advanced platforms, and help craft the overall solution strategy to achieve their business objectives. By leveraging your expertise and understanding of enterprise SaaS and AI Solutions, you will establish yourself as a leader with a valued point of view. With deep Contract Lifecycle Management and Spend Management expertise, you will play a pivotal role in guiding the world's largest companies through their Source-to-Contract evolution—transitioning organizations from legacy processes to AI-augmented workflows and using intelligent contracting to bridge the gap between static agreements and dynamic spend management.       Key Responsibilities:        Architect Complex Spend and Contract Strategies:      Lead technology solutioning for high-stakes opportunities, mapping Workday Strategic Sourcing and Workday CI & CLM (Evisort) into the broader enterprise landscape to achieve specific business outcome, and resolving architecture, security, integration, and AI technology questions during the sales cycle.       Serve as the Technology Thought Leader and  Architectural Bridge:      Act as a strategic point of contract for the product team to design cross-platform integrations, resolving complex technical questions during the sales cycle and establishing the blueprint for how software solutions acquired by Workday unify with core Workday, without assuming responsibility for post-sales implementation.       Lead Executive Technical Overviews:      Act as a strategic advisor to C-Suite stakeholders—including General Counsels, IT Leaders, and Heads of Procurement—translating business needs into technical visions and helping customers scale their use of Evisort.       Map the Source-to-Pay Landscape:      Analyze dependencies between core Workday Procurement and acquired products; develop solution maps that define data flow and architectural "actuals."       Develop and Maintain Resource Bank of Technical Materials:      Build and maintain our own internal reference library of information on under-the-hood platform AI technology, architecture, integration, and security information for use across the organization.       Drive AI Thought Leadership:      Educate customers on the state of AI, explaining how contract-specific LLMs and Custom AI Models can be applied to solve unique challenges in industries like Healthcare, Finance, and High Tech.       Drive Competitive Differentiation:      Partner with sales to articulate the unique value of Evisort’s AI and Strategic Sourcing’s collaborative tools against legacy competitors.       Orchestrate Internal Alignment:      Collaborate with functional SCs, core/specialized AEs, Value Management, and Product Management to ensure a unified customer experience from discovery to technical hand-off.       Manage Technical Compliance & Proposals:    Serve as the pre-sales point person for technical RFI/RFP responses, InfoSec, and Security reviews specifically for Sourcing and CLM, navigating the rigors of legal and data privacy scrutiny.         About Workday’s CLM and Spend Management  Solutions     Workday’s Spend Management suite provides a unified, cloud-native "source-to-pay" ecosystem designed to give organizations total visibility and control over their expenditures. By integrating these solutions, businesses can move away from siloed spreadsheets and legacy systems toward an automated, data-driven approach to procurement.  The suite is highlighted by two primary strategic pillars:       Workday Strategic Sourcing (formerly Scout RFP):      This solution streamlines the entire source-to-contract process. It provides an intuitive, collaborative platform for managing sourcing events (RFPs), reverse auctions, and supplier onboarding. By centralizing communication and data, it allows procurement teams to make faster, more informed purchasing decisions while ensuring full cross-departmental visibility into the pipeline.             Workday Contract Intelligence & CLM (formerly Evisort):      Workday Contract Intelligence & CLM (formerly Evisort) leverages industry-leading AI to transform how organizations handle legal and commercial agreements. By combining advanced AI and OCR to automatically extract key terms and risks from existing contract repositories with a comprehensive end-to-end infrastructure for drafting, negotiating, and executing new ones, the solution turns static documents into actionable strategic data.        About You      The ideal candidate has a strong consulting experience in business applications, coupled with a deep understanding of the CLM or Spend Management Industry.       You are adept at translating business needs into technology solutions. Your exceptional communication and relationship-building skills enable you to engage effectively with C-level executives.       50% travel required       Basic Qualifications:       5+ years of proven experience of enterprise architect, technological consulting, technical solution consulting or similar sales-oriented technical client engagement experience, particularly with senior business or legal professionals.      5+ years of demonstrated ability to diagnose and frame complex problems in an understandable way, prepare and present product demonstrations, decks, and proposals to senior executives, propose actionable solutions and a path forward, and get customer consensus.      3+ experience with CLM or Spend Management         Other Qualifications:       Strong technical background, able to go deep on platform architecture, integrations and APIs, and platform security and guide these discussions with clients.      Understanding of AI technology and Large Language Models and their application to contract and document management or related fields.      Prior experience with law or the legal function within businesses (or technologies serving these functions), with the ability to speak peer-to-peer with senior legal professionals.      Strong strategic vision and the ability to develop and present arguments for AI-driven contract transformation.      Passionate about applying AI to sophisticated legal and business transformation processes.      Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment, with a willingness to pitch in and tackle broad responsibilities beyond core duties to set clients up for success.      Excellent communication and social skills, with the ability to build positive relationships with clients and internal teams.      Passionate about having a broad impact on the company's  growth. description_html: The Opportunity    From the moment you wake up in the morning until you go to bed at night consider the media you consume, the adverts you see, the apps you use, the websites you browse and almost all of the shopping you do online throughout the day. title: Senior OR Principal Enterprise Architect (Security)
description_html: Your work days are brighter here.     We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.           About the Team  The Enterprise Architect team is a strategic partner to Sales and engages with customers and prospects to help them understand how Workdays platform can vastly improve how they manage their people, money, and processes today.  This EA role will support both SLED (State, Local, Higher Education) and Healthcare teams in North America as they evaluate and select Workday as their technology partner.  Our team focuses on HOW Workday is a key enabler of change, digital and AI transformation, and can simplifying an organization’s technology footprint to deliver business impact.    About the Role     As a Senior or Principal Enterprise Architect, you will use your deep technology and security expertise to lead strategic conversations and demonstrate the value of Workday’s platform. You will be a trusted advisor who can translate complex technical and risk concepts into clear business outcomes for both technical and non-technical stakeholders across healthcare, higher education, and state and local government organizations.    You will lead discussions on Workday’s SaaS architecture with a particular emphasis on security, cybersecurity controls, identity and access management, data protection, disaster recovery and business continuity, and data privacy and compliance (including HIPAA and the protection of PHI/PII). You will also articulate how Workday’s integration frameworks, analytics, and AI/ML capabilities support secure, compliant, and resilient operations. In turn, you will deliver high-impact presentations that show how Workday’s architecture and delivery model help customers strengthen their security posture, reduce risk, and achieve better business outcomes.    You will build trusted relationships with CIOs, CISOs, security and compliance leaders, enterprise architects, and business executives. At Workday, we’re committed to helping our colleagues grow, so you will also mentor others by sharing best practices and repeatable patterns from your customer engagements. Your experience and feedback will help shape the evolution of Workday’s technology and security strategy over time.    This role is primarily remote and typically travels as necessary, up to 40–50%.    If you enjoy engaging in thoughtful technology and security conversations, asking insightful questions, and collaborating to solve complex business and risk challenges, this role is for you. We are looking for someone with a deep understanding of enterprise technology and cloud security—either from selling and positioning SaaS solutions or from designing, operating, or governing them. The right candidate delivers compelling presentations and demonstrations with confidence, while actively listening and tailoring the message to the customer’s industry, regulatory environment, and security priorities.    Responsibilities include:    Leading strategic, complex platform and security-focused opportunities, including architecting solution strategies that address cybersecurity, data privacy, HIPAA and PHI protections, and disaster recovery / business continuity requirements.    Delivering relevant technical product presentations, demonstrations, workshops, and executive briefings that highlight Workday’s secure SaaS architecture, zero-trust principles, encryption, access controls, logging/monitoring, and compliance capabilities across healthcare, higher education, and public sector environments.    Identifying and analyzing key systems, dependencies, and integration points within the overall solution landscape, developing solution maps that account for secure data flows, identity management, and protection of regulated data (e.g., PHI and PII).    Providing expert guidance to sales and deal teams on how to differentiate with Workday technology from a security, resiliency, and compliance perspective, including alignment to relevant industry standards and frameworks.    Collaborating across functional groups (Value Management, Services, Customer Success Management, Security & Compliance) to design customer experiences and solution approaches that build long-term, sustainable, and trusted relationships.       About You      About You   You are a seasoned, consultative Enterprise Architect with deep experience in business applications and cloud platforms, and a strong focus on security, data protection, and compliance. You are adept at translating complex business and regulatory requirements (including HIPAA, PHI/PII handling, and industry-specific policies) into secure, scalable technology solutions. Your exceptional communication and relationship-building skills enable you to engage confidently with C-level executives, CISOs, enterprise architects, and business leaders across healthcare, higher education, and state and local government.    Basic Qualifications      Sr Enterprise Architect     5+ years Enterprise Software Solution Consulting experience OR hands-on experience designing, implementing, or supporting enterprise software solutions (SaaS preferred).     5+ years’ experience delivering compelling presentations and demos to executive and technical audiences, both in person and in virtual meetings, clearly communicating technical concepts, security considerations, and strategic initiatives.      5+ years applied working knowledge of cloud architecture and security (e.g., identity and access management, encryption, network security, logging/monitoring), reporting/analytics, and integration standards (APIs, web services, event-driven architectures).      Experience working with customers in regulated environments such as healthcare (including HIPAA and PHI), higher education, and/or state and local government, with the ability to speak credibly about risk, compliance, security, architecture, and governance.        Principal Enterprise Architect     7+ years Enterprise Software Solution Consulting experience OR hands-on experience designing, implementing, or supporting enterprise software solutions (SaaS preferred).     7+ years’ experience delivering compelling presentations and demos to executive and technical audiences, both in person and in virtual meetings, clearly communicating technical concepts, security considerations, and strategic initiatives.      7+ years applied working knowledge of cloud architecture and security (e.g., identity and access management, encryption, network security, logging/monitoring), reporting/analytics, and integration standards (APIs, web services, event-driven architectures).      Experience working with customers in regulated environments such as healthcare (including HIPAA and PHI), higher education, and/or state and local government, with the ability to speak credibly about risk, compliance, security, architecture, and governance. ​       Other Qualifications    Brings a strong point of view on architecture and security and can influence decision making at executive and technical levels. F amiliarity with presales cycles as it relates to SaaS sales.    Demonstrated multi-pillar solution and product knowledge (e.g., HCM, Financials, Student, Planning, or similar enterprise platforms) and how they integrate into broader enterprise landscapes.    Executive presence and relationship-building skills, with a track record of becoming a trusted advisor to CIO, CISO, CTO, and business leadership.    Highly collaborative and team-oriented, able to partner effectively with Sales, Services, Customer Success, Security & Compliance, and Value Management.    Innovative and adaptable, comfortable operating in a fast-paced environment, learning new technologies (including AI/ML), and tailoring architectural approaches to different industries and regulatory requirements.    Certifications such as CISSP, CCSP, CISM, AAISM are a bonus.       Workday Pay Transparency Statement     The annualized base salary ranges for the primary location and any additional locations are listed below.  Workday pay ranges vary based on work location. As a part of the total compensation package, this role may be eligible for the Workday Bonus Plan or a role-specific commission/bonus, as well as annual refresh stock grants. Each candidate’s compensation offer will be based on multiple factors including, but not limited to, geography, experience, skills, job duties, and business need, among other things. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply   spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field   with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter. title: Analytics Platform Engineer
description_html:
Our mission is to automate coding. The first step in our journey is to build the best tool for professional programmers, using a combination of inventive research, design, and engineering. Our organization is very flat, and our team is small and talent dense. We particularly like people who are truth-seeking, passionate, and creative. We enjoy spirited debate, crazy ideas, and shipping code.

About the role

Take data foundations from 0→1 on the bleeding edge of AI data.

As one of Cursor’s first Analytics Platform Engineers, you’ll own the systems that make company-wide data work reliable, secure, and easy to build on. You’ll work hands-on across our data lakehouse architecture to support a fast-growing data team and uniquely data-savvy business stakeholders. You’ll partner with Data, Product, GTM, and AI research teams to turn messy, repeated data needs into durable infrastructure.

Cursor is already operating at enormous scale, but our data platform is still early. This role is for someone who wants to own the low-level foundations: optimizing TB-scale ingestion, improving resource usage and alerting, codifying access control with infra-as-code, and making pragmatic build-vs-buy decisions across the modern data stack.

Example projects

 - Own and optimize the raw data layer: Improve the performance, reliability, and cost profile of TB-scale first-party data ingestion so downstream analysis, experimentation, and ETL are faster and more trustworthy.

 - Scale orchestration for a growing data team: Make Dagster and related orchestration infrastructure reliable, observable, and ergonomic for a large base of data scientists, analytics engineers, and adjacent technical users.

 - Expand and secure agentic data capabilities: Enable new entrypoints and capabilities for agents to do data work, all while keeping security and privacy requirements high.

What you’ll do

 - Own, operate, and improve Cursor’s Databricks and lakehouse infrastructure as the data team size and data volume scales.

 - Build and optimize ingestion systems for first-party product data and 3rd-party business systems.

 - Ensure observability, alerting, and operational standards across all data infrastructure layers.

 - Evaluate and roll out data tooling where it solves real stakeholder needs, including BI platforms, catalogs, ingestion tools, and reverse ETL systems.

 - Partner with technical and non-technical partners to understand recurring data problems and turn them into scalable platform solutions.

You may be a fit if

 - You have 4+ years of full-time data platform engineering experience.

 - You have built up modern data stacks at a low level, not just written jobs on top of it.

 - You have scaled performant ingestion of billions of data per day.

 - You’re the go-to person for data pipeline orchestration infrastructure used by large user bases; Dagster experience is a strong plus.

 - You want to build foundational systems at a company where data-savvy users will immediately push them to their limits.

description_html: To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.      Job Category  Customer Success    Job Details        About Salesforce     Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. description_html: Your work days are brighter here.     We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.         About the Team  As an Enterprise Architect (EA) within our Solution Consulting team, you are the architectural backbone of the client evaluation process and sales cycles. You don’t just explain software; you design the future state for global enterprises. You will bridge the gap between complex technical requirements and transformative business outcomes, challenging the industry status quo at every turn.    About the Role      While a broad technical foundation is required, we believe the best Architects are those who pursue their passions. We provide the autonomy for you to lean into your strengths and evolve your role. Future-Proofing:  Actively contribute to discussions on the "next wave" of innovation, including  AI/ML , enterprise integration strategies, and evolving global security standards.        Cross-Industry Influence:  Support a multifaceted range of industry verticals, communicating the value of our SaaS offering to both highly technical and non-technical audiences.      Deep-Tech Specialization:  Becoming a subject matter expert in global security or advanced application development (Workday Extend).     Strategic Consulting:  Moving beyond the "how" to the "why," partnering with C-suite executives to define long-term digital transformation roadmaps.     Thought Leadership:  Representing the company on the global stage through public speaking, industry conferences, and white papers. Security:  Navigating the intricacies of global compliance and data protection.     Data and AI Architecture:  Ensuring data integrity and scalability across the enterprise.
